clusters.graphs: Meaning lives in graphs — knowledge graphs as the substrate for understanding
clusters.trust: Trustworthy by design — evaluation harnesses, transparency, and ground truth in every AI system
clusters.personal: Personal data, universal patterns — self-tracking and family memory as serious data science

graphs.title: Meaning lives in graphs
graphs.intro: I keep coming back to the same conviction: understanding is a graph — entities, relationships, provenance. These projects test that idea against citations, conversations, and fictional narratives.

trust.title: Trustworthy by design
trust.intro: Most of my projects ship with evaluation built in — ground-truth harnesses, gold-standard validation, capability negotiation, transparency layers. That’s the practice: I don’t ship AI I can’t verify.

personal.title: Personal data, universal patterns
personal.intro: Runs, hives, family stories — personal data treated with production-grade seriousness, because the methods that make sense of one life scale to any messy domain.

projects.name: Digital Twin
projects.tagline: A GraphRAG chatbot that answers as me — and can prove where every answer came from.
projects.description: An AI portfolio chatbot grounded in a Neo4j knowledge graph: 167 canonical entities linked to curated content sections, hybrid vector+graph retrieval with tunable scoring weights, sensitivity tiers gated by passphrase, multi-provider LLM via LiteLLM, production logging with sub-16-microsecond overhead, a 51-test CI suite, and an offline eval harness across 8 question categories. Runs as two live deployments (ChromaDB vs Neo4j backends) from one codebase for A/B comparison.
projects.marketable_angle: Flagship. GraphRAG + eval harness + dual-backend A/B is exactly the production-RAG skill set companies are hiring for in 2026. The 'lessons learned' entries (graph signals vs hallucination) are ready-made LinkedIn posts.
projects.story_hook: I built an AI that interviews on my behalf — then built the evaluation harness to catch it when it lies.
projects.receipt: Hybrid retrieval with documented, tunable scoring weights; a 51-test suite gates every deploy; every query logged with latency, cost, and retrieval scores.

projects.name: Poolula Platform
projects.tagline: An LLM bookkeeper for a real LLC — verified against ground truth, not vibes.
projects.description: AI-powered management platform for a real Colorado rental-property LLC: FastAPI + SQLModel backend, ChromaDB document search, RAG chatbot with safe SELECT-only SQL generation, Airbnb import with accrual accounting, compliance-obligation tracking, and dual evaluation harnesses — including 15 rental-income questions validated against CSV ground truth with 1% numerical tolerance. Currently integrating DSPy and MLflow for pipeline optimization and experiment tracking.
projects.marketable_angle: The strongest 'business value' story in the portfolio: a real company, real money, real compliance deadlines — and an AI you can audit. DSPy/MLflow work-in-progress is highly current content.
projects.story_hook: When the chatbot answers 'what was my rental income in August,' it had better match the spreadsheet to within 1%.
projects.receipt: When the chatbot answers “what was my rental income in August,” it must match the spreadsheet to within 1%. The harness checks — 15 questions against CSV ground truth.

projects.name: Citation Compass
projects.tagline: Predicting which papers will cite each other — with TransE embeddings built from scratch.
projects.description: Academic citation network platform: a from-scratch TransE knowledge-graph-embedding implementation predicts citation links with confidence scores; Neo4j stores the network; graph algorithms detect research communities and centrality; a 4-notebook research pipeline covers training through analysis; Streamlit dashboard with LaTeX/report export.
projects.marketable_angle: Proof of ML depth beyond API calls: implementing TransE from scratch signals you understand embeddings at the math level, not just the import statement. Resonates with research-adjacent roles.
projects.story_hook: Everyone uses embeddings. I implemented the embedding model from the paper to understand what it actually learns.
projects.receipt: From-scratch implementation of TransE knowledge-graph embeddings — not a wrapper, not an API call. The 4-notebook pipeline shows every step.

projects.name: Digital Memory Chest
projects.tagline: Family memories as a knowledge graph — provenance-preserved, Wikidata-enriched, AI-narrated.
projects.description: An AI-powered memorial and family-memory platform: photos, audio, and stories are transcribed (Whisper), tagged (CLIP), and organized into a 14-entity knowledge graph enriched with Wikidata, preserving relationships and provenance across generations. AI generates respectful narratives from collected memories; token-based sharing and moderation support collaborative family contribution.
projects.marketable_angle: The most emotionally resonant project — and a direct embodiment of the Sensemaking thesis. Connects to your father's legacy; powerful as a talk or essay rather than a feature list.
projects.story_hook: Schema is where meaning lives — including in a family's stories. I gave my family's memories a data model.
projects.receipt: A 14-entity knowledge graph with Wikidata enrichment — preserving not just memories but the relationships and provenance between them.

projects.name: ChronoScope
projects.tagline: Documents in, interactive timelines out — with gold-standard precision/recall validation.
projects.description: AI-powered temporal extraction: upload resumes, letters, or narrative documents and LLMs extract events, dates, people, and places into interactive Plotly timelines. Includes confidence scoring, gold-standard validation with quantitative precision/recall, a knowledge-graph view of extracted entities, multi-document processing, and persistent annotation.
projects.marketable_angle: Structured extraction with measured quality is a top enterprise LLM use case. The gold-standard validation angle differentiates it from every 'I made GPT extract stuff' demo.
projects.story_hook: Extraction demos are easy. Knowing your extractor's recall is engineering.
projects.receipt: Every extracted event carries a confidence score, and gold-standard validation reports quantitative precision and recall before you rely on it.

projects.name: Fitness Dashboard
projects.tagline: 14 years of workouts, one classifier, and a dashboard that shows its reasoning.
projects.description: A full ETL-to-insight pipeline for personal fitness data: imports 14+ years of MapMyRun history, an ML classifier separates real runs from dog walks, statistical trend and outlier detection, and an 'algorithm transparency' layer where every insight is clickable to reveal how it was computed, with confidence scores and user feedback loops. AWS Lambda/S3/RDS pipeline with a Streamlit front end, deployed at workouts.barbhs.com.
projects.marketable_angle: The transparency/explainability layer is the marketable part — 'click any insight to see how it was calculated' is a UX pattern enterprises want for AI features. Also your most relatable LinkedIn material (marathoner + data scientist).
projects.story_hook: My classifier had to learn the difference between a marathon training run and walking the dog. Then it had to explain itself.
projects.receipt: Every automated conclusion is one click away from its own methodology — explainability as a UX pattern, not a compliance checkbox.

projects.name: Beehive Analytics
projects.tagline: Four years of hive photos, turned into a queryable knowledge graph with weather context.
projects.description: Computer vision meets beekeeping: inspection photos pass through EXIF extraction, Google Cloud Vision analysis (bee/comb detection), color-palette analysis for comb health, and Open-Meteo weather correlation, producing interactive timelines, calendars, and a queryable record. Dockerized, deployed on Google Cloud Run at hivetracker.barbhs.com.
projects.marketable_angle: Unbeatable as brand content: beekeeper + computer vision is memorable in a way no SaaS demo can be. Strong multimodal-pipeline credentials (EXIF + vision + weather + color).
projects.story_hook: The bees don't keep records. So I taught a vision model to read four years of their history from my photos.
projects.receipt: A real multimodal pipeline — EXIF, computer vision, color analysis, weather correlation — serving a craft I practice every week.

projects.name: ConvoScope
projects.tagline: One chat interface, three LLM providers, zero single points of failure.
projects.description: A multi-provider AI chat application with intelligent failover across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google: when one provider degrades, requests route to the next, transparently. Conversation persistence, session reload, HTML export, and topic extraction; the foundation for later multi-LLM evaluation writing.
projects.marketable_angle: Provider-agnostic architecture is a real enterprise concern (vendor lock-in, outage resilience). Best presented as the origin story of your multi-LLM evaluation practice.
projects.story_hook: The first question every enterprise asks about LLMs: 'what happens when the provider goes down?' I built the answer.
projects.receipt: Provider-agnostic by architecture: when one API degrades, requests route to the next without interrupting the conversation.

projects.name: Naruto Network Graph
projects.tagline: Network science answers what fans argue about: when did the side characters stop mattering?
projects.description: Character co-appearance networks extracted from 426 subtitle files across three Naruto story arcs (87 characters, 36 hand-coded validation relationships). Shannon entropy quantifies ensemble balance, community detection tests whether algorithmic clusters match villages and teams, and centrality tracking measures protagonist dominance over time. Inspired by 'Network of Thrones'; pipeline generalizes to any subtitled series. Neo4j import and visualization in progress.
projects.marketable_angle: Pure virality potential — anime fandom is enormous and data-literate. 'Quantifying when Sakura became irrelevant' is the kind of post that travels far beyond your network. Also quietly demonstrates rigorous pipeline design (hand-coded validation set!).
projects.story_hook: Fans have argued about this for 20 years. I have entropy curves.
projects.receipt: Fans have argued about this for twenty years. I have entropy curves — and a hand-coded ground-truth set of 36 relationships to validate them.

projects.name: Concept Cartographer
projects.tagline: Chat with an AI and watch the conversation become a knowledge graph — live.
projects.description: A Gradio chat where every turn does double duty: a single structured LLM call returns both a conversational answer and an extracted ontology — typed concepts and relationships — that feed a persistent graph growing across the conversation. Coherence filtering keeps the map readable: past 30 nodes, new concepts must connect to something already on the map. Export the graph as JSON or PNG and pipe it into Neo4j, notes, or slides.
projects.receipt: One structured call per turn extracts narrative and ontology simultaneously — roughly half the latency and cost of the usual two-call pattern.
projects.marketable_angle: The Sensemaking thesis as a 60-second live demo — the single best link to put in front of a prospect or hiring manager who asks ‘what do you mean by sensemaking?’
projects.story_hook: Every conversation has a hidden map. This one draws it while you talk.

projects.name: TranscriptWorkbench
projects.tagline: A provider-agnostic transcription workbench that tells you what your provider can’t do.
projects.description: Audio or video in, transcripts out — with OpenAI, AWS Transcribe, and local faster-whisper adapters all mapped to one canonical transcript schema. The distinctive part is capability negotiation: request timestamps, confidence, or speaker diarization, and the app reports honestly whether your selected provider supports them — supported, partial, proxy, or diagnostic — instead of pretending all ASR providers return equivalent outputs. Raw responses are preserved for reproducibility, jobs persist to SQLite, and a pricing service estimates cost per provider before you run.
projects.receipt: A six-state capability matrix (supported → unsupported) replaces the silent pretense that every speech-to-text provider is interchangeable.
projects.marketable_angle: Capability negotiation is an unusually honest design pattern — great LinkedIn essay material on trustworthy AI UX.
projects.story_hook: Most transcription apps lie by omission about what their provider supports. This one negotiates.

projects.name: Midjourney Image Tracker
projects.tagline: Chrome extension that deduplicates and galleries your Midjourney bookmarks.
projects.description: Hover any Midjourney image to bookmark it; smart URL standardization collapses thumbnail and full-size variants of the same image to prevent duplicates; export the collection as an offline HTML gallery or a plain URL list.
projects.receipt: URL standardization collapses every format variant of an image to one canonical key — small tool, real data-modeling discipline.
projects.marketable_angle: Shows JavaScript / browser-extension range beyond the Python stack.

capabilities.name: GraphRAG & Retrieval
capabilities.description: Hybrid retrieval combining vector similarity with knowledge-graph structure; tunable scoring, tier gating, backend A/B comparison.

capabilities.name: Knowledge Graphs
capabilities.description: Modeling domains as entities and relationships in Neo4j — from family memories to citations to beehives. The portfolio's connective tissue.

capabilities.name: Evaluation & Trust
capabilities.description: Eval harnesses, gold-standard validation, ground-truth checking, algorithm transparency, confidence scoring. Appears in 6 of 9 projects — the portfolio's strongest differentiator.

capabilities.name: NLP & Structured Extraction
capabilities.description: Turning unstructured text — documents, subtitles, transcripts — into typed events, entities, and relationships.

capabilities.name: ML & Prediction
capabilities.description: From-scratch TransE embeddings for link prediction; classical classifiers with explainable outputs.

capabilities.name: Network Science
capabilities.description: Centrality, community detection, entropy — quantifying structure in citation networks and fictional narratives alike.

capabilities.name: Computer Vision
capabilities.description: Cloud Vision and CLIP pipelines extracting structure from photographs — hive health, memory tagging.

capabilities.name: Multi-LLM Orchestration
capabilities.description: Provider-agnostic architectures via LiteLLM and custom failover; model comparison as a first-class workflow.

capabilities.name: Data Engineering & ETL
capabilities.description: Ingestion pipelines with provenance tracking: CSV imports, accrual accounting, subtitle parsing, AWS Lambda ETL.

archive.name: CareerCraft (2024)
archive.line: GenAI cover-letter assistant with ATS gap analysis — my first deployed LLM product.

archive.name: IoT Temperature Sensor Fleet (2021)
archive.line: Distributed MQTT sensor network — where I learned real-time streams must be chunked for comprehension.

archive.name: Digital Portfolio Workshop Series (2024)
archive.line: Hands-on sessions codifying a repeatable information architecture for data portfolios — the ancestor of this very page.
